title: Exercises: Error-Correcting Codes
description: Hands-on exercises implementing linear codes and syndrome decoding

exercises.title: Linear Code Implementation
exercises.description: Implement a complete linear code class with encoding and syndrome computation.
exercises.task: Create a LinearCode class with generator matrix, parity-check matrix, and basic operations.

exercises.title: Syndrome Decoder
exercises.description: Implement syndrome-based decoding with precomputed lookup table.
exercises.task: Create a decoder that builds a syndrome table and decodes received words.
exercises.starterCode:

hints: Use combinations to generate all error patterns of weight ≤ max_weight
Store first seen syndrome (minimum weight error)
Decode: lookup syndrome, subtract error from received

exercises.title: Simple ISD Implementation
exercises.task: Create a simple Information Set Decoding solver.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np
from typing import Optional

class SimpleISD:
    def __init__(self, H: np.ndarray, max_iter: int = 1000):
        self.H = H % 2
        self.r, self.n = H.shape
        self.k = self.n - self.r
        self.max_iter = max_iter
    
    def solve(self, syndrome: np.ndarray, target_weight: int) -> Optional[np.ndarray]:

testCases.input: H = np.random.randint(0,2,(4,8)); e = np.array([0,0,1,0,0,0,0,1]); s = H @ e % 2; isd = SimpleISD(H); found = isd.solve(s, 2); found is None or np.sum(found) == 2

hints: Try to invert rightmost r columns
If successful and weight matches, found solution
